title: Flavor Chart Interpretation Study
consentText: **You are invited to participate in a research study on chart interpretation.**
This study evaluates how different chart designs support interpretation of flavor-related data.
You will answer questions about rating distributions, flavor profiles, and population differences.
**Your responses and response times will be recorded.**
**This study evaluates chart readability and task support, not personal ability.**
**Participation is voluntary and you may stop at any time.**
introText: You will see several chart designs that summarize flavor ratings for foods or compare populations of raters.
Some questions ask you to identify which taste attributes are strongly supported by ratings, while others ask you to find foods matching a profile or compare differences between populations.
The study is organized into tutorials, practice trials, main study trials, and short chart rating questions.
**Please answer based only on the information visible in each chart.**
**Work as accurately and quickly as you can.**

backgroundQuestions.label: How familiar are you with charts such as histograms, stacked bars, and radar charts?
How would you describe your data visualization experience?

blocks.title: Block 1: Distribution and Agreement
blocks.intro: In this block, you will identify which combinations of taste attributes are most strongly supported by the ratings. Focus on overall support and agreement in the distributions rather than simply looking for the highest mean.
blocks.taskInstruction: Select the one option that best matches the attributes most strongly supported by the shown ratings.

onboarding.title: Distribution-Aware Radial Profile
callouts: The red outline summarizes the profile, while the shaded radial bands show how ratings are distributed within each taste dimension.

onboarding.title: Histogram Small Multiples
callouts: Each panel shows the full rating distribution for one taste attribute, making support and agreement visible directly.

onboarding.title: Stacked Bar Distribution
callouts: Each stacked bar shows the share of ratings at each level for a taste attribute, from low to high.

practiceTrials.prompt: Which combination of taste attributes is most strongly supported by the ratings?

realTrials.prompt: Which combination of taste attributes is most strongly supported by the ratings?
Which combination of taste attributes is most strongly supported by the ratings?

blocks.title: Block 2: Dominant Profile Similarity
blocks.intro: In this block, you will compare several numbered foods at once and identify which foods match a target flavor profile.
blocks.taskInstruction: Select **exactly 2** numbered foods that match the target profile. Use the chart only, and submit once your two selections are final.

practiceTrials.prompt: Which of the numbered foods match the target flavor profile: Aromatic, Texture, Piquant?

blocks.title: Block 3: Spatial Profile Comparison
blocks.intro: In this block, you will identify foods that match a target profile defined by attributes in different regions of the chart space, such as one attribute on one side and another on the opposite side.
blocks.taskInstruction: Select **exactly 2** numbered foods that match the target spatial profile.

subjectiveSection.title: Part A and B Chart Evaluation
subjectiveSection.instructions: Please rate the charts you used in Blocks 1 to 3.

questions.label: This chart made the requested information easy to identify.
I felt confident in my answers when using this chart.
This chart clearly conveyed the relevant flavor patterns or differences.

blocks.title: Block 4: Distribution Comparison
blocks.intro: In this block, you will compare two populations and identify the taste attributes on which their distributions differ most clearly.
blocks.taskInstruction: Select the one option that best describes where the two populations differ.

onboarding.title: Z-Score Radar Chart
callouts: The shape summarizes which attributes are above or below the reference population, and larger radial displacement indicates larger differences.

onboarding.title: Dual Histogram Comparison
callouts: Each attribute shows two mirrored distributions, letting you compare where each population is concentrated at each rating level.

stimulus.comparisonLabel: Female Participants vs Rest of the Population
Female Participants vs Rest of the Population

practiceTrials.prompt: For these two populations, on which taste attributes do they differ?

blocks.title: Block 5: Distribution Difference Size
blocks.intro: In this block, you will judge whether the differences between two populations are substantial or relatively subtle.
blocks.taskInstruction: Choose whether the shown differences are significant or subtle.
